channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: A father and son are both indicted on murder charges in a mass school shooting in Georgia
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A grand jury indicted both a father and son on murder charges Thursday in a mass shooting at Apalachee High School in Winder, Georgia.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Almost 23% of people say politics influences where they live: Poll
item.description: "Some go so far as to consider moving to find that alignment," Danielle Hale, chief economist for Realtor.com, said.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, KRON4

item.title: Mitzi Gaynor, star of ‘South Pacific,’ dies at 93
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Mitzi Gaynor, the effervescent dancer and actor who starred as Nellie Forbush in the 1958 film "South Pacific" and appeared in other musicals with Bing Crosby, Frank Sinatra and Gene Kelly, has died. She was 93.
item.dc:creator: KAITLYN HUAMANI and BOB THOMAS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Google Flights adds 'Cheapest' option for travelers who just don't care how they get there
item.description: Google Flights has a new option for immediately finding the “cheapest” flight itinerary, but it may come at a slightly different cost.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: What to know about absentee, mail-in, and early voting
item.description: Various forms of casting election ballots have changed since the COVID-19 pandemic affected in-person turnout and voting
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Florida digs out of mountains of sand swept in by back-to-back hurricanes
item.description: BRADENTON BEACH, Fla. (AP) — When a hurricane sets its sights on Florida, storm-weary residents may think of catastrophic wind, hammering rain and dangerous storm surge. Mounds of sand swallowing their homes? Not so much.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 3 states renew efforts to limit abortion pill access
item.description: The lawsuit was filed in a Texas federal court Friday, roughly four months after the Supreme Court decided against undoing changes to the FDA’s regulations on the abortion pill that expanded access — such as allowing it to be mailed without an in-person doctor’s visit.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, KRON4

item.title: Israel says Hamas' top leader Yahya Sinwar was killed in Gaza
item.description: Israel’s foreign minister has confirmed that Israeli troops in Gaza have killed Hamas’ top leader Yahya Sinwar, a chief architect of last year's attack on Israel that sparked the war.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa, Samy Magdy, Jack Jeffery, KTLA 5

item.title: 7-year-old crashes one school bus into another
item.description: She won't be able to drive for another nine years, but the parents of a Missouri second grader say she's already had her first crash. Seven-year-old Alannah Cheffen, of Independence, said she crashed her school bus into another bus on school grounds.
item.dc:creator: Dave D'Marko, KTLA 5

item.title: Gulf War illness may increase risk for heart disease or stroke
item.description: The illness also was associated with more than double the odds of being diagnosed with diabetes and high blood pressure, according to the study, which was published Friday in the Journal of the American Heart Association.
item.dc:creator: Michael Merschel, American Heart Association News, NewsNation

item.title: Small town still healing after losing 20 young men on D-Day
item.description: On July 17, 1944, the small town of Bedford, Virginia with a population of just 3,200 would learn that 20 men died in the D-Day invasion.
item.dc:creator: Marielena Balouris, NewsNation

item.title: Liam Payne died from 'multiple traumas,' 'internal and external bleeding,' autopsy reveals
item.description: Officials in Argentina have released a cause of death for Liam Payne, the former One Direction member who died on Wednesday at the age of 31.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Homeland Security grants temporary status to Lebanese already in the United States
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration on Thursday announced temporary legal status for Lebanese citizens already in the United States, likely pleasing some Arab voters, including in the battleground state of Michigan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 7-year-old crashes one school bus into another during apparent training in Missouri
item.description: She won't be able to drive for another nine years but parents of an Independence second grader say she's already had her first crash.
item.dc:creator: Dave D'Marko, KRON4

item.title: New renderings show how A's ballpark would transform Las Vegas Strip
item.description: Extensive details have been revealed about the proposed $1.5 billion "multi-purpose, enclosed ballpark" on the Las Vegas Strip that could become home to the Las Vegas Athletics MLB franchise in time for the 2028 baseball season.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, Fox 5

item.title: Liam Payne's family releases statement as more details, autopsy results emerge in singer's death
item.description: Payne, 31, died after a fall from the third story of the Casa Sur hotel in Buenos Aires on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Israel's foreign minister confirms that Hamas' top leader Yahya Sinwar was killed in Gaza
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israel’s foreign minister has confirmed that Israeli troops in Gaza have killed Hamas’ top leader Yahya Sinwar, a chief architect of last year's attack on Israel that sparked the war.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, SAMY MAGDY and JACK JEFFERY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Electoral battleground North Carolina starts early in-person voting while recovering from Helene
item.description: ASHEVILLE, N.C. (AP) — Early in-person voting began statewide Thursday in the presidential battleground of North Carolina, including in mountainous areas where thousands of potential voters still lack power and clean running water after Hurricane Helene's epic flooding.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Israel says Hamas' top leader Yahya Sinwar was killed in Gaza
item.description: Israel’s foreign minister has confirmed that Israeli troops in Gaza have killed Hamas’ top leader Yahya Sinwar, a chief architect of last year's attack on Israel that sparked the war.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa, Samy Magdy, Jack Jeffery, Fox 5

item.title: Catholic Archdiocese of L.A. to pay $880 million in childhood sex abuse claims
item.description: A landmark settlement has been reached with the Catholic Archdiocese of Los Angeles to settle more than 1,300 claims of childhood sexual abuse and assault.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, Fox 5

item.title: Person whose DNA was found on collar of dead puppy tied to fire hydrant has died
item.description: After a pit bull puppy was found dead, tied to a fire hydrant in Richmond's East End in November of 2023, investigators have released new information and closed the case.
item.dc:creator: Katelyn Harlow, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Instagram rolls out new features to prevent sextortion scams targeting teens
item.description: Instagram has introduced a series of new safety features to prevent sextortion schemes, marking the platform's latest effort to protect users, especially teenagers, from online harm.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, WGN 9

item.title: Vance says 'no' when asked if Trump lost in 2020
item.description: The answer was the most specific Vance has been on the subject, and a departure from the series of evasions he has offered about the election Trump continues to say without evidence was decided fraudulently.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Helene and Milton are both likely to be $50 billion disasters, joining ranks of most costly storms
item.description: Monstrous hurricanes Helene and Milton caused so much complex havoc that damages are still being added up, but government and private experts say they will likely join the infamous ranks of Katrina, Sandy and Harvey as super costly $50-billion-plus killers.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ex-Las Vegas-area Democratic politician gets at least 28 years in prison for killing reporter
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — A former Las Vegas-area Democratic elected official was sentenced Wednesday to serve at least 28 years in Nevada state prison for killing an investigative journalist who wrote articles critical of his conduct in office two years ago and exposed an intimate relationship with a female
item.dc:creator: KEN RITTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FTC announces rule to make it easier to cancel subscriptions
item.description: The department will soon implement a "final click to cancel rule," a move that will require sellers to make it "as easy for consumers to cancel their enrollment as it was to sign up.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: Biden delivers final eulogy during Ethel Kennedy memorial service in DC
item.description: Ethel Kennedy, 96, died last week. She had been hospitalized after suffering a stroke earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Do you keep money in payment apps? Here's why you probably shouldn't
item.description: As the use of payment apps has grown in recent years, the Consumer Finance Protection Bureau has issued guidance on best practices to avoid pitfalls.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Small business disaster loan program is out of money until Congress approves new funds
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson assured there would be support to provide necessary funds – when Congress returns after the November election.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Where is the 'best restroom' in the U.S.? In a Utah gas station
item.description: Maverik often advertises itself as "Adventure's First Stop" -- but now it can say it's home to "America's Best Restroom," too.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Former Ozzy Osbourne guitarist Jake E. Lee shot while walking dog in Las Vegas
item.description: Guitarist Jake E. Lee, who formerly played for Ozzy Osbourne, was shot Tuesday while walking his dog, his publicist said.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, ABC 4

item.title: North Carolina man shot, killed outside of his own wedding; family wants answers
item.description: Tyrek Burton, 37, was fatally shot outside his own wedding reception at Barber Park Events Center in Greensboro, North Carolina, over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Elijah Skipper, Fox 5

item.title: Man to row across the Pacific Ocean to honor victims of war in Ukraine
item.description: Aurimas Mockus from Lithuania has embarked on a historic journey across the Pacific Ocean from San Diego to Australia in a solo rowing boat, aiming to be the first person in the world to complete the 12,000 kilometer trip alone, without help or stops.
item.dc:creator: Dan Plante, WGN 9

item.title: Coca-Cola recalls 'zero sugar' lemonade after it's found to contain full sugar
item.description: The recall, which Coca-Cola issued in September but didn’t announce publicly, applied to 13,152 cases, each with 12 cartons of 12 cans of 12 ounces of lemonade.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: NHC monitoring 2 disturbances in the Atlantic and Caribbean | Tracking the Tropics
item.description: With Florida's Gulf Coast left reeling after back-to-back major hurricanes, our Tracking the Tropics team explains what could spare the region from experiencing a third storm within a month.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WGN 9

item.title: Convicted killer Susan Smith charged after speaking with filmmaker
item.description: GREENWOOD, S.C. (WSPA) - Convicted killer Susan Smith has been convicted of a disciplinary charge weeks before her parole hearing. Smith, of Union County, was sentenced to life in prison for the murder of her children in Oct. 1994. The SC Department of Corrections confirmed with 7NEWS that on Aug.
item.dc:creator: Nikolette Miller, WGN 9

item.title: FEMA temporarily pauses aid in parts of NC over reported threats
item.description: The Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) temporarily paused aid in parts of North Carolina this weekend after alleged threats targeted the agency’s personnel aiding in recovery efforts following Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WGN 9
